hooks.prompt: Review the Tailwind CSS code change for issues. Check for: 1) Arbitrary values used repeatedly instead of extending the theme (magic numbers — if you use bg-[#1a1b2e] or p-[13px] more than once, add it to tailwind.config as a design token), 2) Missing responsive breakpoint handling (desktop-only styles — add sm:, md:, lg: variants for layout, typography, and spacing that should adapt), 3) Dark mode styles without dark: variant (no dark mode support — add dark: prefix for background, text, and border colors on themed components), 4) Long className strings without component extraction (30+ classes per element — extract to a component or use @apply in component CSS for complex, repeated patterns), 5) Using !important or style prop to override Tailwind (specificity war — fix the cascade instead: reorder classes, use more specific variants, or restructure components), 6) Missing hover, focus, and active states on interactive elements (no visual feedback — add hover:, focus:, focus-visible:, active: variants on buttons and links), 7) Fixed pixel widths instead of responsive utilities (breaks on different screens — use max-w-*, w-full, flex, grid for fluid layouts), 8) Typography without proper text hierarchy (all text looks the same — use consistent heading sizes with text-xl/2xl/3xl, proper line-height with leading-*, and font-weight contrast), 9) Colors used directly instead of semantic tokens (hard to theme — use bg-primary, text-muted-foreground semantic names via CSS variables, not raw bg-blue-500 everywhere), 10) Missing transition utilities on state changes (jarring visual jumps — add transition-colors, transition-all, or duration-200 on elements with hover/focus state changes). Return {"ok": true} if no issues, or {"ok": false, "reason": "specific issue found"} if problems detected.
